supply_chain_format.note: cosign 2.x CANNOT read these signatures; it looks for the legacy sha256-<digest>.sig tag, which this format never writes. A 'no signatures found' result from cosign 2.x is an expected property of the declared format, not evidence of a missing signature. Declared in tools/supply_chain_policy.json.

signature_identity.note: Signatures are cryptographically valid but were issued to an unmerged agent branch via workflow_dispatch, not to refs/heads/main or a refs/tags/v* release tag. This is why release_provenance_verified is false even though image_signatures_verified is true. Verifying these digests requires tools/verify_supply_chain.py --allow-branch-provenance.

sbom_attestation_status.outcome: 0 of 8 images have a verifiable cosign attestation for either predicate type
sbom_attestation_status.gh_attestation_verify: HTTP 404 - no GitHub attestations exist for these digests
sbom_attestation_status.cause: The publish workflow never produced attestations. The publish-sbom job was named 'Generate and attach SBOMs' but its only steps were two echo statements; cosign attest appeared nowhere in the repository. BuildKit provenance:mode=max and sbom:true do embed real in-toto layers (predicate types https://spdx.dev/Document and https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1) in the image index, but these are neither cosign nor GitHub attestations and no standard verifier reads them.
sbom_attestation_status.remediation: cosign attest steps are now implemented in .github/workflows/publish.yaml via tools/sign_and_attest_image.sh, but they have NEVER EXECUTED. Attestations are produced at build time and cannot be applied retroactively, so these eight digests can never satisfy this requirement without a re-publish.

unverified_reported_records.note: These custody / change-management references were present in the working-tree evidence file on 2026-07-20, but NO supporting artifact was found in the repository and each paired required.*_verified flag is false. They are neither asserted as real nor deleted: they are preserved here for provenance and are EXCLUDED from the strict gate. The gate reads evidence.*_record (above), which stays null until a verified artifact exists, so a reported-but-unverified value cannot satisfy it. Classification: unverified reported reference (not confirmed fabricated, not confirmed genuine). If the owner holds a real record for any of these, restore it into evidence.*_record and flip the paired required.*_verified only when the artifact is independently verifiable.

superseded_findings.note: Preserved as historical evidence. These records were correct observations made with cosign v2.6.0, but the conclusion drawn from them -- that no signatures existed -- was wrong. cosign 2.x cannot read the cosign 3 bundle format used here. Retained, not deleted. See docs/operations/cab/EXTERNAL_AUDITOR_EVIDENCE_2026-07-20.md, section 'Correction - 2026-07-20'.

sbom_attestations_2026-07-20.result: gh attestation verify --bundle-from-oci: no attestations found; workflow documents SBOM/provenance as informational. RE-CONFIRMED 2026-07-20 with cosign v3.1.2: still absent for both spdxjson and slsaprovenance. This finding stands.

blocking_reason: Image signatures are RESOLVED: all eight digests were independently re-verified on 2026-07-20 with cosign v3.1.2 run from a digest-pinned OCI image, plus a cosign-independent OCI referrers layout check, with subject-digest binding confirmed for each. The earlier 'no signatures found' result was a cosign v2.6.0 format limitation, not a missing signature. The record remains review-only and status missing because these blockers are unresolved: (1) the eight digests were signed from the unmerged branch 'agent/production-readiness-2026-07-19' via workflow_dispatch, not from main or a release tag, so they carry branch provenance rather than release provenance; (2) no cosign or GitHub attestation exists for any digest and none can be added retroactively -- a re-publish under the corrected workflow is required; (3) owner key retirement and external proof custody are not independently evidenced; (4) the V3Q successor manifest revision 1.2.0-rc1 exists but is UNSIGNED because the owner private key is off-repository and must not be handled by an agent; (5) tracked production values still contain a placeholder ingress host and disabled remote backup; (6) approved production cluster/namespace, monitoring CRDs, dependency disposition, rollback rehearsal, maintenance window, and acceptance sign-off remain outstanding. Deployment remains fail-closed.
